```{r}
            df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]][is.na(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]])] <- mode_val
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    # 4. Predecir con modelo adaptado
    tryCatch({
      predicciones <- predict(modelo_adaptado, newdata = df_visit_para_prediccion, se.fit = TRUE)
      
      predictions[[v]] <- data.frame(
        participant_id = df_visit_para_prediccion$participant_id,
        visit = v,
        cohort = cohort_name,
        predicted_score = predicciones$fit,
        se_fit = predicciones$se.fit,
        df = predicciones$df,
        residual_scale = predicciones$residual.scale,
        n_variables_utilizadas = length(variables_disponibles),
        variables_utilizadas = paste(variables_disponibles, collapse = ", "),
        variables_excluidas = if (length(vars_con_muchos_nas) > 0) {
          paste(vars_con_muchos_nas, collapse = ", ")
        } else {
          "Ninguna"
        },
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      message(paste("✓ Visita", v, ": Predicciones exitosas usando", length(variables_disponibles), 
                   "variables para", nrow(df_visit_para_prediccion), "participantes"))
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("✗ Error en la visita", v, ":", e$message))
    })
  }
  
  # Combinar todas las predicciones
  if (length(predictions) > 0) {
    todas_predicciones <- bind_rows(predictions)
    return(todas_predicciones)
  } else {
    warning("No se pudieron hacer predicciones para ninguna visita")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Función para predecir todas las cohortes
predecir_todas_cohortes <- function(modelo_resultado, datos, visitas = c("M0","M12","M24","M36"),
                                   max_na_percent = 10, cohortes_objetivo = NULL,
                                   cohorte_referencia = "PPMI - Sporadic") {
  
  if (!require(purrr)) install.packages("purrr")
  library(purrr)
  
  if (is.null(cohortes_objetivo)) {
    cohortes_objetivo <- c("Healthy Control", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "SNCA - Aff")
  }
  
  # Tomar solo cohortes objetivo que estén presentes en los datos
  cohortes_presentes <- intersect(unique(datos$mutation), c(cohortes_objetivo, cohorte_referencia))
  
  predicciones_por_cohorte <- map(cohortes_presentes, function(c) {
    # Solo predecir para las cohortes objetivo (excluye la referencia si no está en cohortes_objetivo)
    if (!(c %in% cohortes_objetivo) && c != cohorte_referencia) return(NULL)
    
    datos_filtrados <- datos %>% filter(mutation == c, visit_name %in% visitas)
    if (nrow(datos_filtrados) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    predecir_sobre_cohorte(
      modelo_resultado = modelo_resultado,
      nueva_cohorte = datos_filtrados,
      cohort_name = c,
      visitas = visitas,
      max_na_percent = max_na_percent
    )
  })
  
  bind_rows(predicciones_por_cohorte)
}

# Función para preparar predicciones combinadas
preparar_predicciones_combinadas <- function(resultados_modelo, predicciones_externas, 
                                            cohorte_referencia = "PPMI - Sporadic",
                                            visitas_plot = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")) {
  
  # Preparar predicciones de entrenamiento
  train_preds <- resultados_modelo$training_predictions %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, visit, cohort, predicted_score, actual_score) %>%
    mutate(cohort = ifelse(cohort == "Sporadic", cohorte_referencia, cohort))
  
  # Combinar todas las predicciones
  all_preds <- bind_rows(
    train_preds,
    predicciones_externas %>% mutate(actual_score = NA)
  ) %>%
    filter(visit %in% visitas_plot)
  
  return(all_preds)
}

# =============================================================================
# FUNCIONES DE VISUALIZACIÓN PARA PREDICCIONES
# =============================================================================

# Función para gráfico de cajas general
plot_predicciones_general <- function(all_preds, titulo = "Predicciones de severidad") {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  library(ggplot2)
  
  ggplot(all_preds, aes(x = interaction(cohort, visit), y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
    labs(
      title = titulo,
      x = "Cohorte y Visita",
      y = "Puntuación Predicha"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

# Función para comparar predicciones vs actuales
plot_predicciones_vs_actuales <- function(all_preds, titulo = "Predicciones vs Valores Reales") {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(tidyr)) install.packages("tidyr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(tidyr)
  
  all_preds_long <- all_preds %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(predicted_score, actual_score),
                 names_to = "type",
                 values_to = "score")
  
  ggplot(all_preds_long, aes(x = cohort, y = score, fill = type)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
    labs(
      title = titulo,
      x = "Cohorte",
      y = "Puntuación",
      fill = "Tipo de Puntuación"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

# Función para gráficos por visita con tests estadísticos
plot_predicciones_por_visita <- function(all_preds, cohorte_referencia, cohortes_objetivo,
                                        visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")) {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(ggpubr)) install.packages("ggpubr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggpubr)
  
  visitas_unicas <- unique(all_preds$visit)
  
  for (v in visitas_unicas) {
    datos_visita <- all_preds %>% filter(visit == v)
    
    # Cohortes en esta visita
    cohortes_en_visita <- intersect(unique(datos_visita$cohort), c(cohortes_objetivo, cohorte_referencia))
    
    # Construir lista de comparaciones
    comparisons_visita <- lapply(setdiff(cohortes_en_visita, cohorte_referencia), function(c) {
      c(c, cohorte_referencia)
    })
    
    if (length(comparisons_visita) == 0) {
      message(paste("No hay comparaciones para la visita", v, "- omitiendo test."))
      next
    }
    
    # Crear plot individual
    p_individual <- ggplot(datos_visita %>% filter(cohort %in% c(cohortes_objetivo, cohorte_referencia)),
                           aes(x = cohort, y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Predicción de severidad motora -", v),
        subtitle = paste("Comparaciones: cada cohorte vs", cohorte_referencia),
        x = "Cohorte",
        y = "Puntuación Motora Predicha",
        caption = "Significancia: *** p < 0.001; ** p < 0.01; * p < 0.05; ns = no significativo"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40")
      ) +
      stat_compare_means(
        comparisons = comparisons_visita,
        method = "wilcox.test",
        label = "p.signif"
      )
    
    # Guardar plot individual
    ggsave(
      filename = paste0("cohort_predict_comparison_Motor_", v, ".png"),
      plot = p_individual,
      width = 12,
      height = 8,
      dpi = 300
    )
    message(paste("Plot guardado para visita:", v))
  }
}

# Función para scatter plot de entrenamiento
plot_scatter_entrenamiento <- function(train_preds, cohorte_referencia = "PPMI - Sporadic") {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  library(ggplot2)
  
  ggplot(train_preds, aes(x = actual_score, y = predicted_score)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Puntuación Predicha vs Real en ", cohorte_referencia),
      x = "Puntuación Motora Real",
      y = "Puntuación Motora Predicha"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

# =============================================================================
# FIGURA R5 - COMPARACIÓN DE PREDICCIONES POR COHORTE
# =============================================================================

crear_figura_R5 <- function(all_preds_motor, all_preds_nonmotor, 
                           guardar = TRUE, ancho = 16, alto = 16) {
  
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
  if (!require(ggpubr)) install.packages("ggpubr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggpubr)
  
  # Configuración
  cohort_order <- c("Healthy Control", "SNCA - Aff", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "PPMI - Sporadic")
  cohort_colors <- c(
    "Healthy Control" = "#1b9e77",
    "SNCA - Aff"      = "#d95f02",
    "GBA - Aff"       = "#7570b3",
    "LRRK2 - Aff"     = "#e7298a",
    "PPMI - Sporadic" = "#66a61e"
  )
  cohorte_referencia <- "PPMI - Sporadic"
  visitas_plot <- c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")
  
  # Función auxiliar para plots significativos
  plot_predicciones_significativas <- function(datos_visita, cohorte_ref, colores, orden, ylab = "") {
    
    datos_visita <- datos_visita %>% mutate(cohort = factor(cohort, levels = orden))
    cohortes_a_comparar <- setdiff(levels(datos_visita$cohort), cohorte_ref)
    
    # Calcular p-values y solo mantener significativos
    signif_df <- lapply(cohortes_a_comparar, function(c) {
      g1 <- datos_visita$predicted_score[datos_visita$cohort == c]
      g2 <- datos_visita$predicted_score[datos_visita$cohort == cohorte_ref]
      if (length(g1) > 1 && length(g2) > 1) {
        p <- wilcox.test(g1, g2)$p.value
        if (!is.na(p) && p < 0.05) {
          y_pos <- max(g1, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05 * (max(datos_visita$predicted_score, na.rm = TRUE) -
                                                   min(datos_visita$predicted_score, na.rm = TRUE))
          label <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
                          ifelse(p < 0.01, "**", "*"))
          return(data.frame(cohort = c, ypos = y_pos, label = label))
        }
      }
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    signif_df <- do.call(rbind, signif_df)
    
    # Crear plot base
    p <- ggplot(datos_visita, aes(x = cohort, y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.65, color = "black",
                   outlier.shape = 16, outlier.size = 1.5) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = colores, drop = FALSE) +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = c("HC", "SNCA", "GBA", "LRRK2", "sPD"), drop = FALSE) +    
      labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = unique(datos_visita$visit)) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
    
    # Agregar etiquetas de significancia
    if (!is.null(signif_df) && nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
      p <- p + geom_text(data = signif_df, aes(x = cohort, y = ypos, label = label),
                         inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 7.5)
    }
    
    return(p)
  }
  
  # Generar plots por visita
  plots_motor <- lapply(visitas_plot, function(v) {
    datos_visita <- all_preds_motor %>% filter(visit == v)
    plot_predicciones_significativas(datos_visita, cohorte_referencia, cohort_colors, cohort_order)
  })
  
  plots_nonmotor <- lapply(visitas_plot, function(v) {
    datos_visita <- all_preds_nonmotor %>% filter(visit == v)
    plot_predicciones_significativas(datos_visita, cohorte_referencia, cohort_colors, cohort_order)
  })
  
  # Combinar plots
  col_nonmotor <- ggarrange(plotlist = plots_nonmotor, ncol = 1, nrow = length(visitas_plot))
  col_motor <- ggarrange(plotlist = plots_motor, ncol = 1, nrow = length(visitas_plot))
  
  combined_plot <- ggarrange(col_nonmotor, col_motor, ncol = 2, nrow = 1, widths = c(1, 1))
  
  # Títulos de columnas
  title_nonmotor <- text_grob("Predicted Non-Motor Score", size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  title_motor <- text_grob("Predicted Motor Score", size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  
  titles_row <- ggarrange(title_nonmotor, title_motor, 
                          ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1), 
                          labels = c("A", "B"),
                          label.x = 0.05, label.y = 0.75, hjust = 0, align = "hv")
  
  # Combinar figura final
  final_plot <- ggarrange(titles_row, combined_plot, nrow = 2, heights = c(0.05, 0.8))
  
  # Guardar si se solicita
  if (guardar) {
    ggsave(filename = "Figure_R5.png", plot = final_plot, width = ancho, height = alto, dpi = 300)
    
    # Guardar objetos para reproducibilidad
    save(plots_motor, plots_nonmotor, combined_plot, final_plot,
         plot_predicciones_significativas, cohort_order, cohort_colors, 
         cohorte_referencia, visitas_plot,
         file = "Figure_R5_objects.RData")
  }
  
  return(final_plot)
}

# =============================================================================
# EJECUCIÓN PRINCIPAL - PIPELINE COMPLETO
# =============================================================================

ejecutar_pipeline_predicciones <- function(base_dir, Motor_vars, Non_Motor_vars, 
                                          resultados_modelo_motor = NULL,
                                          resultados_modelo_nonmotor = NULL) {
  
  # 1. Preparar datos de cohortes genéticas
  cat("=== PREPARANDO DATOS DE COHORTES GENÉTICAS ===\n")
  datos_cohortes <- preparar_datos_cohortes_geneticas(base_dir)
  
  # 2. Configurar cohortes de predicción
  config <- configurar_cohortes_prediccion(datos_cohortes)
  datos_filtrados <- config$datos
  cohorte_referencia <- config$cohorte_referencia
  cohortes_objetivo <- config$cohortes_objetivo
  
  cat("Cohortes incluidas en el análisis:", paste(unique(datos_filtrados$mutation), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # 3. Ejecutar predicciones para motor
  if (!is.null(resultados_modelo_motor)) {
    cat("=== EJECUTANDO PREDICCIONES PARA MOTOR ===\n")
    predicciones_motor <- predecir_todas_cohortes(
      modelo_resultado = resultados_modelo_motor,
      datos = datos_filtrados,
      cohortes_objetivo = cohortes_objetivo,
      cohorte_referencia = cohorte_referencia
    )
    
    all_preds_motor <- preparar_predicciones_combinadas(
      resultados_modelo_motor, predicciones_motor, cohorte_referencia
    )
  }
  
  # 4. Ejecutar predicciones para no motor
  if (!is.null(resultados_modelo_nonmotor)) {
    cat("=== EJECUTANDO PREDICCIONES PARA NO MOTOR ===\n")
    predicciones_nonmotor <- predecir_todas_cohortes(
      modelo_resultado = resultados_modelo_nonmotor,
      datos = datos_filtrados,
      cohortes_objetivo = cohortes_objetivo,
      cohorte_referencia = cohorte_referencia
    )
    
    all_preds_nonmotor <- preparar_predicciones_combinadas(
      resultados_modelo_nonmotor, predicciones_nonmotor, cohorte_referencia
    )
  }
  
  # 5. Generar figura R5
  if (!is.null(resultados_modelo_motor) && !is.null(resultados_modelo_nonmotor)) {
    cat("=== GENERANDO FIGURA R5 ===\n")
    figura_R5 <- crear_figura_R5(all_preds_motor, all_preds_nonmotor)
  }
  
  
  # Retornar resultados
  resultados <- list(
    datos_cohortes = datos_filtrados,
    cohorte_referencia = cohorte_referencia,
    cohortes_objetivo = cohortes_objetivo
  )
  
  if (!is.null(resultados_modelo_motor)) {
    resultados$predicciones_motor <- predicciones_motor
    resultados$all_preds_motor <- all_preds_motor
  }
  
  if (!is.null(resultados_modelo_nonmotor)) {
    resultados$predicciones_nonmotor <- predicciones_nonmotor
    resultados$all_preds_nonmotor <- all_preds_nonmotor
  }
  
  if (exists("figura_R5")) {
    resultados$figura_R5 <- figura_R5
  }
  
  return(resultados)
}

# =============================================================================
# EJEMPLO DE USO
# =============================================================================

# Ejemplo de cómo ejecutar el pipeline completo:
#

# 
# # Ejecutar análisis principales primero (usando las funciones del código anterior)
resultados_motor <- ejecutar_analisis_motor(Covariates_score, Motor, Non_Motor)
resultados_nonmotor <- ejecutar_analisis_non_motor(Covariates_score, Non_Motor, Motor)
# 
# # Ejecutar pipeline de predicciones
resultados_predicciones <- ejecutar_pipeline_predicciones(
  base_dir = base_dir,
  Motor_vars = Motor,
  Non_Motor_vars = Non_Motor,
  resultados_modelo_motor = resultados_motor$resultados,
  resultados_modelo_nonmotor = resultados_nonmotor$resultados
)


```






# Selection of the Optimal k for Progression Clustering (Motor & Non-Motor)


```{r}

# *Selection of the Optimal k for Progression Clustering (Motor & Non-Motor)

# Cargar librerías necesarias
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(fpc)
library(clusterSim)

# 1. CALCULAR PERCENTILES - Versión optimizada
severity_scores <- combined_severity_score_all_visits

# Identificar columnas y calcular percentiles en una sola operación
cols_to_use <- grep("Score", names(severity_scores), value = TRUE)

severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
  group_by(Visit) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_use),
                ~ rank(.) / n(),
                .names = "{.col}_percentile")) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. FUNCIÓN OPTIMIZADA PARA EVALUACIÓN DE CLUSTERING
evaluate_clustering_progression <- function(Covariates_score, 
                                           progression_column,
                                           k_range = 2:20) {
  
  results_distances <- data.frame()
  
  for (k in k_range) {
    # Procesamiento de datos en pipeline única
    df_diff <- Covariates_score %>%
      select(participant_id, Visit, all_of(progression_column)) %>%
      arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Visit, values_from = all_of(progression_column), 
                  names_prefix = "Visit_") %>%
      mutate(
        S0 = Visit_0,
        diff_0_12 = Visit_12 - Visit_0,
        diff_12_24 = Visit_24 - Visit_12,
        diff_24_36 = Visit_36 - Visit_24
      ) %>%
      select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)
    
    # Escalado y filtrado
    data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]
    data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0]
    
    if (ncol(data_filtered) == 0) next
    
    data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)
    
    # Clustering
    km_cluster <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)
    cluster_labels <- km_cluster$cluster
    
    # Verificar clusters válidos
    if (any(table(cluster_labels) < 2)) next
    
    # Métricas
    dist_kmeans <- dist(data_scaled, method = "euclidean")
    stats_kmeans <- fpc::cluster.stats(dist_kmeans, cluster_labels)
    db_index_kmeans <- clusterSim::index.DB(data_scaled, cluster_labels, d = dist_kmeans)$DB
    
    results_distances <- rbind(results_distances, data.frame(
      k = k,
      average_within = stats_kmeans$average.within,
      average_between = stats_kmeans$average.between,
      within_between_ratio = stats_kmeans$wb.ratio,
      average_silhouette = stats_kmeans$avg.silwidth,
      davies_bouldin = db_index_kmeans
    ))
  }
  
  return(results_distances)
}

# 3. EVALUACIÓN AUTOMATIZADA PARA TODOS LOS DOMINIOS
percentile_cols <- grep("_percentile$", names(severity_scores), value = TRUE)

# Evaluar solo dominios principales (evitar duplicados raw/mod)
main_domains <- c("Motor", "Non_Motor", "Cognitive", "Neuro", 
                  "Neuro_Cogn", "Sleep", "Non_Motor_Overall")

results_list <- map(main_domains, ~ {
  col_name <- paste0(.x, "_Score_raw_percentile")
  if (col_name %in% percentile_cols) {
    evaluate_clustering_progression(
      Covariates_score = severity_scores,
      progression_column = col_name,
      k_range = 2:20
    )
  }
}) %>% set_names(main_domains) %>% compact()

# 4. FUNCIÓN UNIFICADA PARA ANÁLISIS Y VISUALIZACIÓN
analyze_progression_cluster <- function(severity_scores, score_col, label, k = 2) {
  
  # Procesamiento unificado
  df_diff <- severity_scores %>%
    select(participant_id, Visit, all_of(score_col)) %>%
    arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Visit, values_from = all_of(score_col), 
                names_prefix = "Visit_") %>%
    mutate(
      S0 = Visit_0,
      diff_0_12 = Visit_12 - Visit_0,
      diff_12_24 = Visit_24 - Visit_12,
      diff_24_36 = Visit_36 - Visit_24
    ) %>%
    select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)
  
  # Clustering
  data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]
  data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0]
  data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)
  
  km_cluster <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  df_diff$cluster <- factor(km_cluster$cluster)
  
  # Preparar datos para visualización
  df_long_diff <- df_diff %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36"),
                 names_to = "interval", values_to = "delta") %>%
    mutate(interval = factor(interval, levels = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36")))
  
  # Estadísticas resumen
  summary_stats <- df_long_diff %>%
    group_by(cluster, interval) %>%
    summarise(
      media = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      desviacion = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Gráfico
  cluster_sizes <- table(km_cluster$cluster)
  cluster_labels <- paste0("Cluster ", names(cluster_sizes), " (n=", cluster_sizes, ")")
  
  trajectory_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = interval, y = media, group = cluster, color = cluster)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = media - desviacion, ymax = media + desviacion, fill = cluster), 
                alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(labels = cluster_labels) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(labels = cluster_labels) +
    labs(
      title = paste(label, "Score Trajectories by Cluster"),
      x = "Visit Interval", 
      y = "Percentile Change (Mean ± SD)",
      color = "Cluster", fill = "Cluster"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  # Añadir clusters al dataset original
  df_cluster <- df_diff %>% select(participant_id, cluster)
  Covariates_cluster <- merge(df_cluster, severity_scores, by = "participant_id")
  
  return(list(
    trajectories = trajectory_plot,
    clusters = Covariates_cluster,
    km_model = km_cluster,
    df_diff = df_diff
  ))
}

# 5. ANÁLISIS PARA MOTOR Y NON-MOTOR (los objetos que necesitas)
motor_results <- analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, 
                                           "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", 
                                           "Motor", k = 2)

non_motor_results <- analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, 
                                               "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile", 
                                               "Non-Motor", k = 3)

# 6. CREAR OBJETOS FINALES REQUERIDOS
Covariates_clusterMotor <- motor_results$clusters
Covariates_clusterNonMotor <- non_motor_results$clusters

# 7. GUARDAR OBJETOS ESENCIALES
save(
  severity_scores,
  Covariates_clusterMotor,
  Covariates_clusterNonMotor,
  motor_results,
  non_motor_results,
  file = file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "clustering_results_essential.RData")
)

# Mostrar gráficos
print(motor_results$trajectories)
print(non_motor_results$trajectories)


```


# Predicting Progression 

Ver cómo afecta a la predicción la inclusión de variables confounder: variables que causen el input(expresión de genes) y el output (predicción).

Las predicciones que se van a analizar son:
- Cambio de percentil
- Grupos de progresión


Las posibles variables confounder que se analizarán serán:
- 


```{r eval=FALSE}

# FUNCIÓN UNIFICADA PARA PREDICCIÓN DE CLUSTERS
evaluate_clusters_with_covariate_ranger <- function(
  severity_scores,
  expression_data,
  covariates_data = NULL,
  clinical_covar = NULL,
  score_type = c("Motor", "Non_Motor"),  # Nuevo parámetro para seleccionar dominio
  k = 3,
  test_size = 0.2,
  n_bootstrap = 10,
  n_cores = 2,
  mtry_select = c(60, 30, 10),
  splitrule_select = "gini",
  min.node.size_select = 10,
  ntrees_select = 500
) {
  # Validar parámetro score_type
  score_type <- match.arg(score_type)
  
  # Determinar columna de score basado en el tipo
  score_column <- switch(score_type,
    "Motor" = "Motor_Score_raw_percentile",
    "Non_Motor" = "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile"
  )
  
  # Librerías necesarias
  require(caret)
  require(doParallel)
  require(dplyr)
  require(tidyr)
  require(ranger)
  require(foreach)
  require(tibble)
  
  registerDoParallel(cores = n_cores)
  
  # ---- 1. Preparar datos de expresión ----
  PPMI_exp_M0_id <- expression_data %>%
    mutate(
      sample_id = rownames(.),
      participant_id = as.numeric(sub("PP-(\\d+)-.*", "\\1", sample_id))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample_id)
  
  # ---- 2. Preparar datos clínicos si se proporcionan ----
  if (!is.null(clinical_covar) && !is.null(covariates_data)) {
    covariates_processed <- covariates_data %>%
      mutate(participant_id = as.numeric(sub("PP-(\\d+)-.*", "\\1", sample_id))) %>%
      select(participant_id, sample_id, all_of(clinical_covar)) %>%
      distinct()
  } else {
    covariates_processed <- NULL
  }

  # ---- 3. Clustering sobre el score seleccionado ----
  df_long <- severity_scores[, c("participant_id", "Visit", score_column)]
  df_long_cluster <- df_long[order(df_long$participant_id, df_long$Visit), ]
  
  df_wide <- pivot_wider(
    df_long_cluster,
    names_from = Visit,
    values_from = all_of(score_column),
    names_prefix = "Visit_"
  )
  
  df_diff <- df_wide %>%
    mutate(
      S0 = Visit_0,
      diff_0_12 = Visit_12 - Visit_0,
      diff_12_24 = Visit_24 - Visit_12,
      diff_24_36 = Visit_36 - Visit_24
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)
  
  # Escalado y filtrado de variables constantes
  data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]
  removed_vars <- names(data_to_scale)[apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) == 0]
  if (length(removed_vars) > 0) {
    cat("Variables sin variación:", removed_vars, "\n")
  }
  
  data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) != 0]
  data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)
  
  # Clustering k-means
  set.seed(123)
  km_cluster <- kmeans(data_scaled, k, nstart = 25)
  df_diff$cluster <- factor(km_cluster$cluster)
  
  cluster_df <- df_diff %>%
    select(participant_id, cluster) %>%
    mutate(cluster = make.names(cluster))
  
  # ---- 4. Filtrado de genes altamente correlacionados ----
  cor_matrix <- cor(PPMI_exp_M0_id %>% select(-participant_id))
  cor_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_matrix))
  high_cor <- cor_long %>% filter(Freq > 0.95, Var1 != Var2)
  genes_to_remove <- unique(c(high_cor$Var1, high_cor$Var2))
  genes_remaining <- setdiff(colnames(PPMI_exp_M0_id %>% select(-participant_id)), genes_to_remove)
  
  PPMI_exp_selected <- PPMI_exp_M0_id %>%
    select(participant_id, all_of(genes_remaining)) %>%
    rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id")
  
  # ---- 5. Construcción del dataset final ----
  df_model <- PPMI_exp_selected %>%
    inner_join(cluster_df, by = "participant_id")
  
  if (!is.null(clinical_covar) && !is.null(covariates_processed)) {
    df_model <- df_model %>%
      inner_join(covariates_processed %>% select(-participant_id), by = "sample_id")
  }
  
  df_model <- df_model %>%
    select(-c(sample_id, participant_id)) %>%
    mutate(cluster = factor(cluster))
  
  # Transformar covariable si es binaria
  if (!is.null(clinical_covar)) {
    if (is.factor(df_model[[clinical_covar]]) || is.character(df_model[[clinical_covar]])) {
      levels_cov <- unique(na.omit(df_model[[clinical_covar]]))
      if (length(levels_cov) == 2) {
        df_model[[clinical_covar]] <- ifelse(df_model[[clinical_covar]] == levels_cov[1], 0, 1)
      } else {
        stop("La covariable categórica tiene más de 2 niveles (excluyendo NA).")
      }
    } else {
      message(paste0("La covariable '", clinical_covar, "' ya es numérica."))
    }
  }

  # ---- 6. Split train/test ----
  set.seed(123)
  train_idx <- createDataPartition(df_model$cluster, p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
  train_data <- df_model[train_idx, ]
  test_data  <- df_model[-train_idx, ]
  
  tune_grid <- expand.grid(
    mtry = mtry_select,
    splitrule = splitrule_select,
    min.node.size = min.node.size_select
  )

  # ---- 7. Bootstrap interno para tuning ----
  cat("Iniciando bootstrap interno para selección de hiperparámetros...\n")
  bootstrap_tuning <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = rbind,
                              .packages = c("ranger", "caret", "dplyr")) %dopar% {
    tryCatch({
      idx <- sample(1:nrow(train_data), replace = TRUE)
      train_boot <- train_data[idx, ]
      oob_idx <- setdiff(1:nrow(train_data), unique(idx))
      test_boot <- train_data[oob_idx, ]
      
      rf_tune <- train(
        x = train_boot %>% select(-cluster),
        y = train_boot$cluster,
        method = "ranger",
        metric = "Accuracy",
        tuneGrid = tune_grid,
        trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 3),
        num.trees = ntrees_select,
        importance = "permutation",
        always.split.variables = clinical_covar
      )
      
      best <- rf_tune$bestTune
      
      if (nrow(test_boot) > 0) {
        pred <- predict(rf_tune, newdata = test_boot)
        acc <- confusionMatrix(pred, test_boot$cluster)$overall["Accuracy"]
      } else {
        acc <- NA
      }
      
      data.frame(
        bootstrap = i,
        mtry = best$mtry,
        splitrule = best$splitrule,
        min.node.size = best$min.node.size,
        Accuracy = acc
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      data.frame(bootstrap = i, mtry = NA, splitrule = NA, min.node.size = NA, Accuracy = NA)
    })
  }

  # ---- 8. Seleccionar mejor conjunto de hiperparámetros ----
  best_params <- bootstrap_tuning %>%
    group_by(mtry, splitrule, min.node.size) %>%
    summarise(MeanAcc = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(MeanAcc)) %>%
    slice(1)

  cat("Mejores hiperparámetros seleccionados:\n")
  print(best_params)

  # ---- 9. Entrenamiento final ----
  rf_final <- ranger(
    formula = cluster ~ .,
    data = train_data,
    mtry = best_params$mtry,
    splitrule = best_params$splitrule,
    min.node.size = best_params$min.node.size,
    num.trees = ntrees_select,
    importance = "permutation",
    always.split.variables = clinical_covar
  )

  # ---- 10. Evaluación final ----
  pred_final <- predict(rf_final, data = test_data)$predictions
  final_cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_final, test_data$cluster)

  if (is.matrix(final_cm$byClass)) {
    balanced_accuracy_test <- mean(final_cm$byClass[,"Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    balanced_accuracy_test <- final_cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  }

  # ---- 11. IC 95% de métricas bootstrap ----
  acc_values <- na.omit(bootstrap_tuning$Accuracy)
  acc_ci <- quantile(acc_values, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)

  # ---- 12. Importancia de variables ----
  var_imp <- tibble::enframe(rf_final$variable.importance, name = "Variable", value = "Importance") %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))

  # ---- 13. Resultado final ----
  list(
    ScoreType = score_type,
    K = k,
    BestParams = best_params,
    FinalModel = rf_final,
    FinalConfusionMatrix = final_cm,
    BalancedAccuracyTest = balanced_accuracy_test,
    BootstrapTuning = bootstrap_tuning,
    AccuracyCI95 = acc_ci,
    VariableImportance = var_imp
  )
}

# FUNCIÓN PARA EJECUTAR MÚLTIPLES EXPERIMENTOS
run_multiple_experiments <- function(
  severity_scores,
  expression_data,
  score_type = c("Motor", "Non_Motor"),
  k_range = 2:7,
  n_boots = 25,
  n_cores = 4,
  save_prefix = "progression_prediction_RF"
) {
  
  score_type <- match.arg(score_type)
  results_list <- list()
  
  for (k in k_range) {
    cat("Ejecutando experimento para", score_type, "k =", k, "\n")
    
    result <- evaluate_clusters_with_covariate_ranger(
      severity_scores = severity_scores,
      expression_data = expression_data,
      covariates_data = NULL,
      clinical_covar = NULL,
      score_type = score_type,
      k = k,
      test_size = 0.2,
      n_bootstrap = n_boots,
      n_cores = n_cores
    )
    
    # Guardar resultado individual
    filename <- paste0(save_prefix, "_k", k, "_", score_type, "_reactome.rds")
    saveRDS(result, filename)
    
    results_list[[paste0("k", k)]] <- result
    
    # Mostrar resumen
    cat("Resultados para k =", k, ":\n")
    print(result$FinalConfusionMatrix$overall["Accuracy"])
    cat("---\n")
  }
  
  return(results_list)
}

# GUARDAR DATOS DE ENTRADA
saveRDS(severity_scores, "severity_scores_for_pred_K")
saveRDS(PPMI_exp_M0, "PPMI_exp_M0_for_pred_K")

# EJECUTAR EXPERIMENTOS PARA MOTOR
cat("=== EJECUTANDO EXPERIMENTOS PARA MOTOR ===\n")
motor_results <- run_multiple_experiments(
  severity_scores = severity_scores,
  expression_data = PPMI_exp_M0,
  score_type = "Motor",
  k_range = 2:5,
  n_boots = 25,
  n_cores = 4,
  save_prefix = "progression_prediction_RF"
)

# EJECUTAR EXPERIMENTOS PARA NON-MOTOR
cat("=== EJECUTANDO EXPERIMENTOS PARA NON-MOTOR ===\n")
non_motor_results <- run_multiple_experiments(
  severity_scores = severity_scores,
  expression_data = PPMI_exp_M0,
  score_type = "Non_Motor",
  k_range = 2:7,
  n_boots = 25,
  n_cores = 4,
  save_prefix = "progression_prediction_RF"
)


```


### Load Models

```{r}

# FUNCIÓN MEJORADA PARA CARGAR MODELOS
load_models <- function(score_type = c("Motor", "Non_Motor"), k_range = NULL, base_path = ".") {
  score_type <- match.arg(score_type)
  
  # Si no se especifica k_range, usar rangos por defecto según el tipo
  if (is.null(k_range)) {
    k_range <- switch(score_type,
      "Motor" = 2:5,
      "Non_Motor" = 2:7
    )
  }
  
  models <- list()
  cat("Cargando modelos para", score_type, "...\n")
  
  for (k in k_range) {
    filename <- switch(score_type,
      "Motor" = paste0("progression_prediction_RF_k", k, "_Motor_reactome.rds"),
      "Non_Motor" = paste0("progression_prediction_RF_k", k, "_Non_Motor_reactome.rds")
    )
    
    file_path <- file.path(base_path, filename)
    
    if (file.exists(file_path)) {
      models[[paste0("k", k)]] <- readRDS(file_path)
      cat("✓ Cargado:", filename, "\n")
    } else {
      cat("✗ No encontrado:", filename, "\n")
      models[[paste0("k", k)]] <- NULL
    }
  }
  
  return(models)
}

# FUNCIÓN PARA RESUMIR MODELOS CARGADOS
summarize_models <- function(models_list, score_type) {
  cat("=== RESUMEN PARA", toupper(score_type), "===\n\n")
  
  for (model_name in names(models_list)) {
    model <- models_list[[model_name]]
    if (!is.null(model)) {
      cat("---", model_name, "---\n")
      cat("Balanced Accuracy:", round(model$BalancedAccuracyTest, 4), "\n")
      cat("Accuracy:", round(model$FinalConfusionMatrix$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")
      cat("Kappa:", round(model$FinalConfusionMatrix$overall["Kappa"], 4), "\n")
      cat("Clases:", paste(levels(model$FinalConfusionMatrix$table), collapse = ", "), "\n")
      cat("\n")
    }
  }
}

# FUNCIÓN PARA COMPARAR MÉTRICAS ENTRE MODELOS
compare_models <- function(models_list) {
  metrics_df <- data.frame()
  
  for (model_name in names(models_list)) {
    model <- models_list[[model_name]]
    if (!is.null(model)) {
      metrics_df <- rbind(metrics_df, data.frame(
        Model = model_name,
        BalancedAccuracy = model$BalancedAccuracyTest,
        Accuracy = model$FinalConfusionMatrix$overall["Accuracy"],
        Kappa = model$FinalConfusionMatrix$overall["Kappa"],
        N_Classes = nrow(model$FinalConfusionMatrix$table),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  return(metrics_df)
}

# USO PRÁCTICO - CARGA Y ANÁLISIS EN UN SOLO PASO

# 1. Cargar todos los modelos
motor_models <- load_models("Motor", k_range = 2:5)
non_motor_models <- load_models("Non_Motor", k_range = 2:7)

# 2. Resumen rápido
summarize_models(motor_models, "Motor")
summarize_models(non_motor_models, "Non_Motor")

# 3. Comparación entre modelos
motor_comparison <- compare_models(motor_models)
non_motor_comparison <- compare_models(non_motor_models)

print("Comparación Motor:")
print(motor_comparison)

print("Comparación Non-Motor:")
print(non_motor_comparison)

# 4. Acceso individual a modelos específicos (manteniendo tu sintaxis original)
# Para acceder a un modelo específico:
results_NoCov_Motor_k2 <- motor_models$k2
results_NoCov_Motor_k3 <- motor_models$k3
results_NoCov_Motor_k4 <- motor_models$k4

results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k2 <- non_motor_models$k2
results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k3 <- non_motor_models$k3
results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k4 <- non_motor_models$k4
results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k5 <- non_motor_models$k5
results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k6 <- non_motor_models$k6
results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k7 <- non_motor_models$k7

# 5. Visualización de comparaciones (opcional)
plot_model_comparison <- function(comparison_df, title) {
  ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Model)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = BalancedAccuracy, color = "Balanced Accuracy"), size = 3) +
    geom_point(aes(y = Accuracy, color = "Accuracy"), size = 3) +
    labs(title = title, y = "Métrica", x = "Modelo") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Balanced Accuracy" = "blue", "Accuracy" = "red")) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_model_comparison(motor_comparison, "Comparación de Modelos - Motor")
plot_model_comparison(non_motor_comparison, "Comparación de Modelos - Non-Motor")

```



# Important genes to predict progression

```{r}

# UNIFIED VARIABLE IMPORTANCE ANALYSIS FUNCTION
analyze_variable_importance <- function(results_object, 
                                       domain_name = "Motor", 
                                       k_value = 2,
                                       additional_vars = 1000) {
  
  # Load required libraries
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  
  # Extract and prepare variable importance data
  var_imp <- results_object$VariableImportance %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
  
  # Scale to 0-100
  var_imp <- var_imp %>%
    mutate(
      Importance = 100 * (Importance - min(Importance)) / (max(Importance) - min(Importance))
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
  
  # Calculate elbow point mathematically (curvature method)
  calculate_elbow_point <- function(var_imp) {
    importances <- var_imp$Importance
    cumulative_sum <- cumsum(importances)
    cumulative_percentage <- cumulative_sum / sum(importances)
    
    n_vars <- length(importances)
    coordinates <- cbind(1:n_vars, cumulative_percentage)
    start_point <- coordinates[1, ]
    end_point <- coordinates[n_vars, ]
    
    distances <- apply(coordinates, 1, function(point) {
      numerator <- abs((end_point[2] - start_point[2]) * point[1] - 
                        (end_point[1] - start_point[1]) * point[2] + 
                        end_point[1] * start_point[2] - end_point[2] * start_point[1])
      denominator <- sqrt((end_point[2] - start_point[2])^2 + (end_point[1] - start_point[1])^2)
      numerator / denominator
    })
    
    elbow_point <- which.max(distances)
    
    return(list(
      elbow_index = elbow_point,
      n_variables = elbow_point,
      cumulative_importance = cumulative_percentage[elbow_point]
    ))
  }
  
  # Apply curvature method
  elbow <- calculate_elbow_point(var_imp)
  
  # Create THREE datasets:
  # 1. Important variables only (above cutoff)
  important_vars <- var_imp[1:elbow$n_variables, ]
  
  # 2. Extended view for plotting (cutoff + additional_vars)
  n_show <- min(elbow$n_variables + additional_vars, nrow(var_imp))
  var_imp_filtered <- var_imp[1:n_show, ]
  
  # Create plot with selected variables
  p <- ggplot(var_imp_filtered, aes(x = Importance, y = reorder(Variable, Importance))) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
    # Horizontal line for elbow point cutoff
    geom_hline(yintercept = nrow(var_imp_filtered) - elbow$n_variables + 0.5, 
               color = "red", linetype = "solid", linewidth = 1.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    # Annotation for elbow line
    annotate("text", 
             x = max(var_imp_filtered$Importance) * 0.7, 
             y = nrow(var_imp_filtered) - elbow$n_variables/2,
             label = paste("Cutoff point:", elbow$n_variables, "variables"), 
             color = "red", size = 4, fontface = "bold", hjust = 0) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Most Important Variables for", domain_name, "Progression Prediction"),
      subtitle = paste("Top", n_show, "variables (criteria: curvature method + next", additional_vars, "variables)"),
      x = "Relative Importance (0-100)",
      y = "Variables"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "darkred", face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
    )
  
  # Show summary
  cat("===", domain_name, "DOMAIN - SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Cutoff point (elbow):", elbow$n_variables, "variables\n")
  cat("Cumulative importance:", round(elbow$cumulative_importance * 100, 1), "%\n")
  cat("Important variables (above cutoff):", nrow(important_vars), "\n")
  cat("Variables shown in plot:", n_show, "\n")
  cat("Additional variables shown:", n_show - elbow$n_variables, "\n")
  cat("Total variables available:", nrow(var_imp), "\n\n")
  
  # Return results
  return(list(
    plot = p,
    important_vars = important_vars,           # Only variables above cutoff
    var_imp_filtered = var_imp_filtered,       # Extended view for plotting
    elbow_point = elbow$n_variables,
    cumulative_importance = elbow$cumulative_importance,
    top_variables = important_vars$Variable    # Only the important ones
  ))
}

# FUNCTION TO CONVERT ENSEMBL IDs TO GENE SYMBOLS
convert_ensembl_to_symbols <- function(var_imp_data, mart = NULL) {
  if (is.null(mart)) {
    library(biomaRt)
    mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
  }
  
  ensembl_ids <- var_imp_data$Variable
  gene_map <- getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "hgnc_symbol"),
                    filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
                    values = ensembl_ids,
                    mart = mart)
  
  # Create new gene symbols
  new_genes <- sapply(ensembl_ids, function(id) {
    symbol <- gene_map$hgnc_symbol[match(id, gene_map$ensembl_gene_id)]
    ifelse(symbol != "" & !is.na(symbol), symbol, id)
  })
  
  var_imp_data$Variable <- new_genes
  var_imp_data$Gene_Symbol <- new_genes
  var_imp_data$Ensembl_ID <- ensembl_ids
  
  return(var_imp_data)
}

# ANALYZE MOTOR DOMAIN
cat("ANALYZING MOTOR DOMAIN...\n")
motor_results <- analyze_variable_importance(
  results_object = results_NoCov_Motor_k2,
  domain_name = "Motor",
  k_value = 2,
  additional_vars = 1000
)

print(motor_results$plot)

# ANALYZE NON-MOTOR DOMAIN  
cat("ANALYZING NON-MOTOR DOMAIN...\n")
non_motor_results <- analyze_variable_importance(
  results_object = results_NoCov_Non_Motor_k3,
  domain_name = "Non-Motor",
  k_value = 3,
  additional_vars = 1000
)

print(non_motor_results$plot)

# OPTIONAL: Convert Ensembl IDs to Gene Symbols (ONLY IMPORTANT VARIABLES)
cat("CONVERTING ENSEMBL IDs TO GENE SYMBOLS (IMPORTANT VARIABLES ONLY)...\n")
library(biomaRt)
ensembl_mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

# Convert only the important variables (above cutoff)
motor_important_genes <- convert_ensembl_to_symbols(motor_results$important_vars, ensembl_mart)
non_motor_important_genes <- convert_ensembl_to_symbols(non_motor_results$important_vars, ensembl_mart)

# Show top genes for each domain (ONLY IMPORTANT ONES)
cat("\n=== TOP IMPORTANT GENES - MOTOR DOMAIN ===\n")
print(head(motor_important_genes[, c("Gene_Symbol", "Importance")], 10))
cat("Total important genes in Motor domain:", nrow(motor_important_genes), "\n")

cat("\n=== TOP IMPORTANT GENES - NON-MOTOR DOMAIN ===\n")
print(head(non_motor_important_genes[, c("Gene_Symbol", "Importance")], 10))
cat("Total important genes in Non-Motor domain:", nrow(non_motor_important_genes), "\n")

# COMPARISON SUMMARY
cat("\n=== COMPARISON SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Motor Domain:\n")
cat("  - Important variables:", motor_results$elbow_point, "\n")
cat("  - Cumulative importance:", round(motor_results$cumulative_importance * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  - Top gene:", motor_important_genes$Gene_Symbol[1], "\n\n")

cat("Non-Motor Domain:\n")
cat("  - Important variables:", non_motor_results$elbow_point, "\n")
cat("  - Cumulative importance:", round(non_motor_results$cumulative_importance * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  - Top gene:", non_motor_important_genes$Gene_Symbol[1], "\n")

# Save important genes to variables for further use
VarImpMotor <- motor_important_genes$Variable
VarImpNonMotor <- non_motor_important_genes$Variable

cat("\n=== DATASET SIZES ===\n")
cat("Motor genes (important only):", length(VarImpMotor), "variables\n")
cat("Non-Motor genes (important only):", length(VarImpNonMotor), "variables\n")


```


# Differential expression analysis


```{r}

analyze_differential_expression <- function(data, 
                                          group_column, 
                                          metadata_columns = c("sample_id"), 
                                          visits = c("M0"),
                                          base_column_name = "cluster") {
  
  # Load required libraries
  if (!require(DESeq2)) install.packages("DESeq2"); library(DESeq2)
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr"); library(dplyr)
  if (!require(biomaRt)) install.packages("biomaRt"); library(biomaRt)
  if (!require(tibble)) install.packages("tibble"); library(tibble)
  
  # 1. Filter data for selected visits
  filtered_data <- data[data$visit_name %in% visits, ]
  
  # Ensure group column is factor
  filtered_data[[group_column]] <- factor(filtered_data[[group_column]])
  
  # 2. Extract count matrix (assuming genes start from column 4)
  countData <- t(as.matrix(filtered_data[, 4:ncol(filtered_data)]))
  countData <- as.matrix(countData)
  
  # 3. Ensembl ID to Gene Symbol translation
  ensembl <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
  ensembl_ids <- rownames(countData)
  
  gene_map <- getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "hgnc_symbol"),
                    filters = "ensembl_gene_id", 
                    values = ensembl_ids,
                    mart = ensembl)
  
  new_rownames <- sapply(ensembl_ids, function(id) {
    symbol <- gene_map$hgnc_symbol[match(id, gene_map$ensembl_gene_id)]
    ifelse(symbol != "" & !is.na(symbol), symbol, id)
  })
  
  rownames(countData) <- new_rownames
  countData <- as.matrix(countData)
  
  # 4. Prepare metadata for DESeq2
  group_column_clean <- gsub(paste0("^", base_column_name, "_"), "", group_column)
  metadata <- filtered_data[, c(metadata_columns, group_column)]
  colnames(metadata)[colnames(metadata) == group_column] <- "group"
  
  # 5. Create DESeqDataSet object
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = countData,
    colData = metadata,
    design = ~ group
  )
  
  # 6. Determine number of groups and execute appropriate analysis
  num_groups <- length(levels(filtered_data[[group_column]]))
  
  if (num_groups == 2) {
    # Analysis for 2 groups (like the first case)
    dds <- DESeq(dds)
    
    # Get group levels
    group_levels <- levels(filtered_data[[group_column]])
    
    # Compare group 1 vs 2
    res <- results(dds, contrast = c("group", as.character(group_levels[1]), 
                                    as.character(group_levels[2])))
    
    # Convert to data.frame and add annotations
    res_df <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
      mutate(
        significant = ifelse(
          padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 0.5, "DEG", "No DEG"
        ),
        overexpressed_in = case_when(
          log2FoldChange > 0.5 & padj < 0.05 ~ paste("Group", group_levels[1]),
          log2FoldChange < -0.5 & padj < 0.05 ~ paste("Group", group_levels[2]), 
          TRUE ~ "None"
        ),
        comparison = paste(group_levels[1], "vs", group_levels[2]),
        cluster_type = group_column_clean
      )
    
    return(list(results = res_df, analysis_type = "2_groups"))
    
  } else if (num_groups >= 3) {
    # Analysis for 3 or more groups (like the second case)
    
    # Helper function for DESeq and results
    get_DEGs <- function(dds, group_var, contrast, label, cluster_type){
      dds_tmp <- dds
      colData(dds_tmp)$grp <- factor(colData(dds_tmp)[[group_var]])
      design(dds_tmp) <- ~ grp
      dds_tmp <- DESeq(dds_tmp)
      
      res <- results(dds_tmp, contrast = c("grp", contrast[1], contrast[2]))
      res_df <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
        rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
        mutate(
          comparison = label,
          cluster_type = cluster_type,
          significant = ifelse(!is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 0.5, "DEG", "No DEG"),
          overexpressed_in = case_when(
            log2FoldChange > 0.5 & padj < 0.05 ~ contrast[1],
            log2FoldChange < -0.5 & padj < 0.05 ~ contrast[2],
            TRUE ~ "None"
          )
        )
      return(res_df)
    }
    
    # Prepare combined groups for comparisons
    group_levels <- levels(filtered_data[[group_column]])
    
    # Create all possible 1 vs rest combinations
    combined_results <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_along(group_levels)) {
      current_group <- group_levels[i]
      remaining_groups <- paste(group_levels[-i], collapse = "+")
      
      # Create grouped variable
      colData(dds)[[paste0("group_", current_group, "_vs_rest")]] <- 
        ifelse(colData(dds)$group == current_group, 
               as.character(current_group), 
               remaining_groups)
      
      # Execute analysis for this comparison
      res_temp <- get_DEGs(dds, 
                          paste0("group_", current_group, "_vs_rest"), 
                          c(as.character(current_group), remaining_groups),
                          paste(current_group, "vs", remaining_groups),
                          group_column_clean)
      
      combined_results[[i]] <- res_temp
    }
    
    # Combine all results
    res_all <- bind_rows(combined_results)
    
    return(list(results = res_all, analysis_type = "multi_groups"))
    
  } else {
    stop("At least 2 groups are required for analysis")
  }
}




### Filtrado por gene_match (genes protein coding)

# We need the raw counts instead of the VST:
PPMI_raw_counts <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "1aFase_ReducirGenes", "PPMI_exp")) %>% 
  rownames_to_column(.data = ., "sample_id") %>%
  filter(sample_id %in% rownames(PPMI_exp_M0)) %>% 
  select(all_of(c("sample_id", gene_match))) 




# Para el análisis de expresion diferencial cogemos solo los genes y las etiquetas motor

genes_motor <- Covariates_clusterMotor %>%
  select(sample_id, cluster, visit_name) %>%
  inner_join(
    PPMI_raw_counts,
    by = "sample_id"
  )



genes_non_motor <- Covariates_clusterNonMotor %>%
  select(sample_id, cluster, visit_name) %>%
  inner_join(
    PPMI_raw_counts,
    by = "sample_id"
  )


# For motor analysis (2 groups)
motor_DEG_results <- analyze_differential_expression(
  data = genes_motor,
  group_column = "cluster",
  visits = c("M0")
)

# For non-motor analysis (3 groups)
non_motor_DEG_results <- analyze_differential_expression(
  data = genes_non_motor,
  group_column = "cluster", 
  visits = c("M0")
)


# Access result dataframes
motor_df_DEG <- motor_DEG_results$results %>% filter(significant == "DEG")
non_motor_df_DEG <- non_motor_DEG_results$results %>% filter(significant == "DEG")

# Summary of DEGs by comparison


```


## Figure R4

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(UpSetR)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
library(png)


# Create gene sets list (replace var_imp with your actual variable importance data)
gene_sets_list <- list(
  "RF_Motor" = VarImpMotor,  # Replace with your RF motor features
  "DEG_Motor" = motor_df_DEG$gene,
  "RF_NonMotor" = VarImpNonMotor,  # Replace with your RF non-motor features
  "DEG_NonMotor" = non_motor_df_DEG$gene
)

# =============================================================================
# COLORS
# =============================================================================
color_rf <- "#FF6B00"       # Orange for Random Forest
color_deg <- "#00A8FF"      # Blue for DEG
color_cluster1 <- "#E41A1C" # Red
color_cluster2 <- "#377EB8" # Blue
color_cluster3 <- "#4DAF4A" # Green
color_none <- "gray70"

# =============================================================================
# VOLCANO PLOTS DATA PREPARATION
# =============================================================================

# Motor volcano data
volcano_motor_data <- motor_DEG_results$results %>%
  mutate(
    method = "DEG",
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        significant == "DEG" & overexpressed_in == "Group 1" ~ "Cluster 1",
        significant == "DEG" & overexpressed_in == "Group 2" ~ "Cluster 2",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Not significant")
    )
  )

top_10_motor <- motor_DEG_results$results %>%
  filter(significant == "DEG") %>%
  group_by(overexpressed_in) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(log2FoldChange), n = 10) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        overexpressed_in == "Group 1" ~ "Cluster 1",
        overexpressed_in == "Group 2" ~ "Cluster 2",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Not significant")
    )
  )

# Non-motor volcano data
volcano_non_motor_data <- non_motor_DEG_results$results %>%
  mutate(
    method = "DEG",
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        significant == "DEG" & str_detect(comparison, "^1 vs") ~ "Cluster 1",
        significant == "DEG" & str_detect(comparison, "^2 vs") ~ "Cluster 2",
        significant == "DEG" & str_detect(comparison, "^3 vs") ~ "Cluster 3",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3", "Not significant")
    )
  )

top_10_non_motor <- non_motor_DEG_results$results %>%
  filter(significant == "DEG") %>%
  group_by(comparison) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(log2FoldChange), n = 10) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        str_detect(comparison, "^1 vs") ~ "Cluster 1",
        str_detect(comparison, "^2 vs") ~ "Cluster 2", 
        str_detect(comparison, "^3 vs") ~ "Cluster 3",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3", "Not significant")
    )
  )

# =============================================================================
# COMMON AXIS LIMITS
# =============================================================================
common_y_limits <- range(
  c(-log10(volcano_motor_data$padj), -log10(volcano_non_motor_data$padj)),
  na.rm = TRUE
)
common_y_limits[2] <- common_y_limits[2] * 1.05

common_x_limits <- range(
  c(volcano_motor_data$log2FoldChange, volcano_non_motor_data$log2FoldChange),
  na.rm = TRUE
)
common_x_limits <- common_x_limits * 1.1  # Add some padding

# =============================================================================
# VOLCANO PLOTS
# =============================================================================
volcano_motor <- ggplot(volcano_motor_data, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significance, alpha = significance), size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Cluster 1" = color_cluster1, "Cluster 2" = color_cluster2, "Not significant" = color_none),
    name = "Overexpressed in",
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Cluster 1" = 0.8, "Cluster 2" = 0.8, "Not significant" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_motor %>% filter(significance != "Not significant"),
    aes(label = gene, color = significance),
    size = 3, box.padding = 0.4, max.overlaps = 20, segment.color = "grey50", segment.size = 0.3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = common_y_limits) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = common_x_limits) +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-Log10(Adjusted p-value)", title = "Motor Clusters") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

volcano_non_motor <- ggplot(volcano_non_motor_data, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significance, alpha = significance), size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Cluster 1" = color_cluster1, "Cluster 2" = color_cluster2, "Cluster 3" = color_cluster3, "Not significant" = color_none),
    name = "Overexpressed in", 
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("Cluster 1" = 0.8, "Cluster 2" = 0.8, "Cluster 3" = 0.8, "Not significant" = 0.3), guide = "none") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_non_motor %>% filter(significance != "Not significant"),
    aes(label = gene, color = significance),
    size = 3, box.padding = 0.4, max.overlaps = 20, segment.color = "grey50", segment.size = 0.3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = common_y_limits) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = common_x_limits) +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-Log10(Adjusted p-value)", title = "Non-Motor Clusters") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# =============================================================================
# HEATMAP & UPSET DATA
# =============================================================================
# Prepare data for heatmap
all_genes <- unique(unlist(gene_sets_list))
heatmap_data <- sapply(gene_sets_list, function(x) all_genes %in% x) * 1
rownames(heatmap_data) <- all_genes

heatmap_ggplot_data <- as.data.frame(heatmap_data) %>%
  mutate(Gene = all_genes) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Gene, names_to = "Analysis", values_to = "Presence")

# Filter: Only genes that appear in at least 2 methods
genes_in_multiple <- rowSums(heatmap_data) >= 2
heatmap_ggplot_data_filtered <- heatmap_ggplot_data %>%
  filter(Gene %in% all_genes[genes_in_multiple])

# Reorder genes by number of analyses they appear in (descending)
gene_counts <- rowSums(heatmap_data)
gene_order <- all_genes[order(gene_counts, decreasing = TRUE)]

# Create heatmap only with genes in at least 2 analyses
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(heatmap_ggplot_data_filtered, 
                       aes(x = Analysis, y = factor(Gene, levels = gene_order))) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = as.factor(Presence)), color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "white", "1" = "#2E86AB"), 
                    labels = c("0" = "Absent", "1" = "Present")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("RF_Motor" = "RF\nMotor",
                              "DEG_Motor" = "DEG\nMotor", 
                              "RF_NonMotor" = "RF\nNon-Motor",
                              "DEG_NonMotor" = "DEG\nNon-Motor")) +
  labs(x = "Analysis", y = "Genes", fill = "Presence", title = "Gene Overlap Heatmap") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# UpSet plot
names(gene_sets_list) <- c("RF Motor", "DEG Motor", "RF Non-Motor", "DEG Non-Motor")
png("temp_upset.png", width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
upset(fromList(gene_sets_list),
      nsets = 4,
      nintersects = 12,
      order.by = "freq",
      mainbar.y.label = "Number of Genes",
      sets.x.label = "Genes per Method",
      sets.bar.color = c(color_rf, color_deg, color_rf, color_deg),
      matrix.color = "black",
      main.bar.color = "steelblue",
      matrix.dot.alpha = 0.7,
      text.scale = c(1.5, 1.2, 1.5, 1.0, 2.0, 2.0)
)
dev.off()

upset_plot_gg <- ggplot() +
  annotation_custom(
    grid::rasterGrob(png::readPNG("temp_upset.png"), width = unit(1, "npc"), height = unit(1, "npc")),
    xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf
  ) +
  labs(title = "Gene Set Intersections") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12))

# =============================================================================
# COMBINE FIGURES
# =============================================================================
combined_figure <- 
  (volcano_non_motor | volcano_motor) /
  ((heatmap_plot | upset_plot_gg) + plot_layout(widths = c(0.8, 1.2))) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = list(c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D'))) &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

ggsave("Figure_R4.png",
       plot = combined_figure,
       width = 16, height = 14, units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white")

if(file.exists("temp_upset.png")) file.remove("temp_upset.png")

# =============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# =============================================================================
gene_summary <- data.frame(
  Analysis = names(gene_sets_list),
  Number_of_Genes = sapply(gene_sets_list, length)
)
print(gene_summary)

# Overlaps between analyses
cat("\nGene overlap between analyses:\n")
for(i in 1:(length(gene_sets_list)-1)) {
  for(j in (i+1):length(gene_sets_list)) {
    overlap <- length(intersect(gene_sets_list[[i]], gene_sets_list[[j]]))
    overlap_pct <- round(overlap / length(gene_sets_list[[i]]) * 100, 1)
    cat(paste0(names(gene_sets_list)[i], " vs ", names(gene_sets_list)[j], ": ", 
               overlap, " genes (", overlap_pct, "% overlap)\n"))
  }
}

# Heatmap summary
cat("\nHeatmap summary:\n")
cat("Total unique genes across all analyses:", length(all_genes), "\n")
cat("Genes in at least 2 analyses:", sum(genes_in_multiple), "\n")
distribution <- table(rowSums(heatmap_data))
for(i in 1:length(distribution)) {
  cat("  - In", as.numeric(names(distribution)[i]), "analyses:", distribution[i], "genes\n")
}



```






# Enriquecimiento

## Plan A

```{r eval=FALSE}

# UNIFIED PATHWAY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS FUNCTION FOR MULTIPLE GROUPS
perform_comprehensive_enrichment_multigroup <- function(gene_sets_list, 
                                                       domain_name = "MultiGroup_Comparison",
                                                       top_terms = 10) {
  
  # Load required libraries
  library(clusterProfiler)
  library(org.Hs.eg.db)
  library(enrichplot)
  library(DOSE)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
  
  # =============================================================================
  # PREPARE GENE SETS IN COMPARECLUSTER FORMAT
  # =============================================================================
  
  # Create the combined dataset with group information
  prepare_gene_data <- function(gene_sets_list) {
    
    # Create empty list to store dataframes
    all_groups <- list()
    
    # Process each gene set
    for (group_name in names(gene_sets_list)) {
      genes <- gene_sets_list[[group_name]]
      
      # Create dataframe for this group
      group_df <- data.frame(
        gene_symbol = genes,
        group = group_name,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      all_groups[[group_name]] <- group_df
    }
    
    # Combine all groups
    combined_df <- bind_rows(all_groups)
    
    # Convert gene symbols to Entrez IDs
    combined_df$entrez <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db,
                                 keys = combined_df$gene_symbol,
                                 column = "ENTREZID",
                                 keytype = "SYMBOL",
                                 multiVals = "first")
    
    # Remove NAs and return
    combined_df <- combined_df[!is.na(combined_df$entrez), ]
    return(combined_df)
  }
  
  # Prepare the data
  gene_data <- prepare_gene_data(gene_sets_list)
  
  cat("GENE SETS PREPARED:\n")
  for (group_name in names(gene_sets_list)) {
    cat(group_name, "genes:", sum(gene_data$group == group_name), 
        "(original:", length(gene_sets_list[[group_name]]), ")\n")
  }
  cat("Total genes with Entrez IDs:", nrow(gene_data), "\n\n")
  
  # =============================================================================
  # GENE ONTOLOGY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS
  # =============================================================================
  
  cat("PERFORMING GENE ONTOLOGY ENRICHMENT...\n")
  
  # Biological Process
  go_bp <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ group,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO",
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
    readable = TRUE
  )
  
  # Molecular Function
  go_mf <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ group,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO", 
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "MF",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
    readable = TRUE
  )
  
  # Cellular Component
  go_cc <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ group,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO",
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "CC",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH", 
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
    readable = TRUE
  )
  
  # =============================================================================
  # KEGG PATHWAY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS
  # =============================================================================
  
  cat("PERFORMING KEGG PATHWAY ENRICHMENT...\n")
  
  kegg_enrichment <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ group,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichKEGG",
    organism = "hsa",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1
  )
  
  # =============================================================================
  # REACTOME PATHWAY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS
  # =============================================================================
  
  cat("PERFORMING REACTOME PATHWAY ENRICHMENT...\n")
  
  # Check if ReactomePA is available
  if (require(ReactomePA, quietly = TRUE)) {
    reactome_enrichment <- compareCluster(
      entrez ~ group,
      data = gene_data,
      fun = "enrichPathway",
      organism = "human",
      pAdjustMethod = "BH",
      pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
      qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
      readable = TRUE
    )
  } else {
    cat("ReactomePA not available, skipping Reactome enrichment\n")
    reactome_enrichment <- NULL
  }
  
  # =============================================================================
  # CREATE ENHANCED DOTPLOTS
  # =============================================================================
  
  create_enhanced_dotplot <- function(enrichment_result, title, top_n = top_terms) {
    if (is.null(enrichment_result) || nrow(as.data.frame(enrichment_result)) == 0) {
      return(ggplot() + 
               geom_text(aes(x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "No significant terms found"), 
                         size = 6) +
               theme_void() +
               ggtitle(title))
    }
    
    # Simplify GO results to reduce redundancy
    if (grepl("GO", title)) {
      enrichment_result <- tryCatch({
        simplify(enrichment_result, cutoff = 0.7, by = "p.adjust", select_fun = min)
      }, error = function(e) {
        enrichment_result
      })
    }
    
    # Create dotplot
    dotplot(enrichment_result, 
            showCategory = top_n,
            font.size = 10,
            title = title) +
      scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", 
                           name = "P-value\n(-log10)") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
        legend.position = "right",
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      )
  }
  
  # Generate all dotplots
  cat("GENERATING DOTPLOTS...\n")
  
  bp_plot <- create_enhanced_dotplot(go_bp, "Gene Ontology - Biological Process")
  mf_plot <- create_enhanced_dotplot(go_mf, "Gene Ontology - Molecular Function") 
  cc_plot <- create_enhanced_dotplot(go_cc, "Gene Ontology - Cellular Component")
  kegg_plot <- create_enhanced_dotplot(kegg_enrichment, "KEGG Pathway Enrichment")
  
  if (!is.null(reactome_enrichment)) {
    reactome_plot <- create_enhanced_dotplot(reactome_enrichment, "Reactome Pathway Enrichment")
  }
  
  # =============================================================================
  # COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY STATISTICS
  # =============================================================================
  
  cat("\n=== COMPREHENSIVE ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
  cat("Analysis:", domain_name, "\n\n")
  
  # Gene set statistics
  gene_summary <- gene_data %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    summarise(
      n_genes = n(),
      original_count = map_dbl(group, ~length(gene_sets_list[[.x]])),
      conversion_rate = n_genes / original_count
    )
  
  print(gene_summary)
  
  # Enrichment statistics
  cat("\nENRICHMENT RESULTS:\n")
  cat("Biological Process GO:", nrow(as.data.frame(go_bp)), "significant terms\n")
  cat("Molecular Function GO:", nrow(as.data.frame(go_mf)), "significant terms\n") 
  cat("Cellular Component GO:", nrow(as.data.frame(go_cc)), "significant terms\n")
  cat("KEGG Pathways:", nrow(as.data.frame(kegg_enrichment)), "significant pathways\n")
  
  if (!is.null(reactome_enrichment)) {
    cat("Reactome Pathways:", nrow(as.data.frame(reactome_enrichment)), "significant pathways\n")
  }
  
  # =============================================================================
  # TOP ENRICHED TERMS COMPARISON
  # =============================================================================
  
  extract_top_terms <- function(enrichment_result, database) {
    result_df <- as.data.frame(enrichment_result)
    if (nrow(result_df) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    result_df %>%
      group_by(Cluster) %>%
      slice_min(p.adjust, n = 5) %>%
      select(Cluster, Description, p.adjust, Count, GeneRatio) %>%
      mutate(Database = database)
  }
  
  # Extract top terms from all databases
  top_terms_list <- list(
    extract_top_terms(go_bp, "GO_BP"),
    extract_top_terms(go_mf, "GO_MF"),
    extract_top_terms(go_cc, "GO_CC"),
    extract_top_terms(kegg_enrichment, "KEGG")
  )
  
  if (!is.null(reactome_enrichment)) {
    top_terms_list[[5]] <- extract_top_terms(reactome_enrichment, "Reactome")
  }
  
  # Combine all top terms
  all_top_terms <- bind_rows(top_terms_list)
  
  if (!is.null(all_top_terms) && nrow(all_top_terms) > 0) {
    cat("\nTOP ENRICHED TERMS ACROSS DATABASES:\n")
    print(all_top_terms)
  }
  
  # =============================================================================
  # RETURN RESULTS
  # =============================================================================
  
  results_list <- list(
    gene_data = gene_data,
    go_bp = go_bp,
    go_mf = go_mf, 
    go_cc = go_cc,
    kegg = kegg_enrichment,
    plots = list(
      bp_plot = bp_plot,
      mf_plot = mf_plot,
      cc_plot = cc_plot,
      kegg_plot = kegg_plot
    ),
    top_terms = all_top_terms,
    summary = gene_summary
  )
  
  if (!is.null(reactome_enrichment)) {
    results_list$reactome <- reactome_enrichment
    results_list$plots$reactome_plot <- reactome_plot
  }
  
  return(results_list)
}

# =============================================================================
# EXECUTE THE ANALYSIS WITH YOUR 4 GROUPS
# =============================================================================

cat("STARTING COMPREHENSIVE PATHWAY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS FOR 4 GROUPS...\n")

# Perform the enrichment analysis
enrichment_results <- perform_comprehensive_enrichment_multigroup(
  gene_sets_list = gene_sets_list,
  domain_name = "Motor_vs_NonMotor_RF_DEG_Comparison",
  top_terms = 10
)

saveRDS(enrichment_results, "enrichment_results_PPMI")

```



```{r}

enrichment_results <- readRDS("enrichment_results_PPMI")

# Display all plots
cat("\nDISPLAYING ENRICHMENT PLOTS...\n")
print(enrichment_results$plots$bp_plot)
print(enrichment_results$plots$mf_plot) 
print(enrichment_results$plots$cc_plot)
print(enrichment_results$plots$kegg_plot)

if (!is.null(enrichment_results$plots$reactome_plot)) {
  print(enrichment_results$plots$reactome_plot)
}

# =============================================================================
# SAVE RESULTS
# =============================================================================

cat("SAVING RESULTS...\n")

# Save dotplots
ggsave("FourGroup_Comparison_GO_BP.png", enrichment_results$plots$bp_plot, 
       width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("FourGroup_Comparison_GO_MF.png", enrichment_results$plots$mf_plot,
       width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300) 
ggsave("FourGroup_Comparison_GO_CC.png", enrichment_results$plots$cc_plot,
       width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("FourGroup_Comparison_KEGG.png", enrichment_results$plots$kegg_plot,
       width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)

if (!is.null(enrichment_results$plots$reactome_plot)) {
  ggsave("FourGroup_Comparison_Reactome.png", enrichment_results$plots$reactome_plot,
         width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
}

# Save top terms to CSV
if (!is.null(enrichment_results$top_terms)) {
  write.csv(enrichment_results$top_terms, "FourGroup_Enrichment_Top_Terms.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}

# Save gene data
write.csv(enrichment_results$gene_data, "FourGroup_Gene_Data_For_Enrichment.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!\n")
cat("Results saved to current directory.\n")

```


## Plan B

```{r eval=FALSE}

# FUSED DOMAIN ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS FUNCTION
perform_fused_domain_enrichment <- function(gene_sets_list, 
                                           domain_name = "Fused_Domain_Comparison",
                                           top_terms = 10) {
  
  # Load required libraries
  library(clusterProfiler)
  library(org.Hs.eg.db)
  library(enrichplot)
  library(DOSE)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
  
  # =============================================================================
  # FUSE GROUPS BY DOMAIN
  # =============================================================================
  
  fuse_groups_by_domain <- function(gene_sets_list) {
    
    # Extract and combine Motor groups
    motor_genes <- unique(c(
      gene_sets_list[["RF Motor"]],
      gene_sets_list[["DEG Motor"]]
    ))
    
    # Extract and combine Non-Motor groups
    non_motor_genes <- unique(c(
      gene_sets_list[["RF Non-Motor"]],
      gene_sets_list[["DEG Non-Motor"]]
    ))
    
    # Create fused gene sets list
    fused_gene_sets <- list(
      "Motor_Domain" = motor_genes,
      "NonMotor_Domain" = non_motor_genes
    )
    
    cat("FUSED DOMAIN STATISTICS:\n")
    cat("Motor Domain (RF + DEG):", length(motor_genes), "unique genes\n")
    cat("  - RF Motor:", length(gene_sets_list[["RF Motor"]]), "genes\n")
    cat("  - DEG Motor:", length(gene_sets_list[["DEG Motor"]]), "genes\n")
    cat("  - Overlap:", length(intersect(gene_sets_list[["RF Motor"]], 
                                        gene_sets_list[["DEG Motor"]])), "genes\n")
    
    cat("Non-Motor Domain (RF + DEG):", length(non_motor_genes), "unique genes\n")
    cat("  - RF Non-Motor:", length(gene_sets_list[["RF Non-Motor"]]), "genes\n")
    cat("  - DEG Non-Motor:", length(gene_sets_list[["DEG Non-Motor"]]), "genes\n")
    cat("  - Overlap:", length(intersect(gene_sets_list[["RF Non-Motor"]], 
                                        gene_sets_list[["DEG Non-Motor"]])), "genes\n\n")
    
    return(fused_gene_sets)
  }
  
  # Fuse the groups
  fused_gene_sets <- fuse_groups_by_domain(gene_sets_list)
  
  # =============================================================================
  # PREPARE GENE SETS IN COMPARECLUSTER FORMAT
  # =============================================================================
  
  # Create the combined dataset with domain information
  prepare_gene_data <- function(fused_gene_sets) {
    
    # Create empty list to store dataframes
    all_domains <- list()
    
    # Process each domain
    for (domain_name in names(fused_gene_sets)) {
      genes <- fused_gene_sets[[domain_name]]
      
      # Create dataframe for this domain
      domain_df <- data.frame(
        gene_symbol = genes,
        domain = domain_name,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      all_domains[[domain_name]] <- domain_df
    }
    
    # Combine all domains
    combined_df <- bind_rows(all_domains)
    
    # Convert gene symbols to Entrez IDs
    combined_df$entrez <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db,
                                 keys = combined_df$gene_symbol,
                                 column = "ENTREZID",
                                 keytype = "SYMBOL",
                                 multiVals = "first")
    
    # Remove NAs and return
    combined_df <- combined_df[!is.na(combined_df$entrez), ]
    return(combined_df)
  }
  
  # Prepare the data
  gene_data <- prepare_gene_data(fused_gene_sets)
  
  cat("FUSED DOMAIN GENE SETS PREPARED:\n")
  for (domain_name in names(fused_gene_sets)) {
    cat(domain_name, "genes:", sum(gene_data$domain == domain_name), 
        "(original unique:", length(fused_gene_sets[[domain_name]]), ")\n")
  }
  cat("Total genes with Entrez IDs:", nrow(gene_data), "\n\n")
  
  # =============================================================================
  # GENE ONTOLOGY ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS
  # =============================================================================
  
  cat("PERFORMING GENE ONTOLOGY ENRICHMENT...\n")
  
  # Biological Process
  go_bp <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ domain,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO",
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
    readable = TRUE
  )
  
  # Molecular Function
  go_mf <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ domain,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO", 
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "MF",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff = 0.1,
    readable = TRUE
  )
  
  # Cellular Component
  go_cc <- compareCluster(
    entrez ~ domain,
    data = gene_data,
    fun = "enrichGO",
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    ont = "CC",
```
